ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-05-2021 Continued To Aug-17-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Specially Appearing Defendant Jennifer Leigh Walts Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash
Off Calendar Apr-20-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT JENNIFER WALTS' Motion To Quash.

No appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Specially appearing defendant Ms. Walts's motion to quash for lack of service is granted.
"When a defendant challenges the court's personal jurisdiction on the ground of improper service of process the burden is on the plaintiff to prove the existence of jurisdiction by proving, inter alia, the facts requisite to an effective service." (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 413.) Plaintiff meets her burden. The proof of service filed by plaintiff "included the declaration of a registered process server averring that he personally served defendant" at the 701 China Basin Street address. (See American Express Centurion Bank v. Zara (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 383, 390.) "Because of the statutory presumption [pursuant to Evid. Code section 647], defendant was thus required to produce evidence that he was not served." (Id.)

Defendant provides a signed declaration declaring that she is a North Carolina resident, was not present at the China Basin address on the date on which she was purportedly served, has not been present at that address since prior to 2019, and has never been served with any papers relating to this action. (Walts Decl. 2-3.) That declaration is sufficient to overcome the presumption and meet her burden. The Court rejects plaintiff's claim that the declaration is unduly vague.

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
